_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill to evaluate the capabilities, limitations and tasking methodologies of organic, theater, national, coalition and other collection capabilities.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-003 (TF, cosine: 0.510), TF-A-001 (TF, cosine: 0.501).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.497). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [capabilities, coalition, collection, evaluate, limitations, methodologies] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.858 (P=0.854, R=0.863). Balanced P/R — the 18-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Skill to evaluate the capabilities, limitations and tasking methodologies of org" and "Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business questio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.520 ≈ STS 0.497 (gap 0.023). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.3%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [capabilities, coalition, collection, evaluate, limitations, methodologies]. WIDAI terms: [ability, analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing].
